title: Today's AI Brief: OpenAI named a Leader in enterprise coding agents by Gartner
summary: OpenAI leads in enterprise AI coding agents, AdventHealth uses ChatGPT for Healthcare

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm taking a look at some fresh news in the world of AI. Today, I've got two interesting stories to share with you. First up, OpenAI has been named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Enterprise AI Coding Agents. This is a big deal, as it recognizes OpenAI's innovation and ability to deploy at an enterprise scale with its Codex technology.

So, what does it mean to be a Leader in this space? Essentially, Gartner is acknowledging that OpenAI is a key player in helping businesses automate coding tasks. This can save time, reduce errors, and free up developers to focus on more complex problems. I'm guessing it's like having a super-smart coding assistant that's always on the job.

In other news, AdventHealth is using ChatGPT for Healthcare to streamline workflows and reduce administrative burden. This allows their staff to spend more time on patient care, which is great news for everyone involved. I mean, who doesn't want their healthcare team to have more time to focus on, you know, actually helping people?

It's interesting to see how AI is being used in different industries. From coding to healthcare, AI is making a real impact. My read is that we're just starting to scratch the surface of what's possible with AI, and it's exciting to think about what the future holds.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
